TextENGNew: I learned that the fourth year of Oyashiro-sama's curse had struck when Sis called me the morning after the Watanagashi.
changeReason: New translation adds oyashiro, altering character/location references.

TextENGNew: The victim was the spiteful aunt who had been tormenting Satoshi-kun and Satoko.
Since Satoshi-kun's suffering stemmed from the relationship between the aunt and Satoko, the aunt's death meant the problem was solved.
I should have been dancing with joy -- as indecent as that would be.@kBut I didn't feel that way.
``...Do the police have any leads on who did it?``
When I murmured that, Sis fell silent on the other end.
``Who knows.@k@vS06/03/150300173. That bastard Ooishi seems to be canvassing the neighborhood pretty aggressively.@k@vS06/03/150300174. He seems convinced the culprit was someone close to her...``
Sis and I are identical twins.@kBeing the same person, we think the same thoughts.
So just as I was vaguely considering the possibility that it was Satoshi-kun who killed the aunt, Sis was surely thinking the same thing.
Which meant I could skip saying the obvious part out loud.
``...Does Satoshi-kun have an alibi for that night...?``
``Huh? Uh, mm... I wonder...``
``Hahhh...``
I let out a deep, exasperated sigh.
``Are you an idiot?@k@vS06/06/150600818. Satoshi-kun was at the festival.@k@vS06/06/150600819. That's what you should be telling them.``
``Oh... yeah... you're right.@k@vS06/03/150300177. Sorry, I didn't think of that...``
I added one more sigh for good measure.
This fool of a sister had known full well that Satoshi-kun would be an obvious suspect the moment the aunt turned up dead, and yet she'd done absolutely nothing about establishing an alibi for him.
changeReason: New translation adds satoshi, satoshi-kun, altering character/location references.

TextENGNew: ``...So, then...@k@vS06/03/150300179. ...does that mean... Satoshi actually...?``
``Whether Satoshi-kun did it or not isn't the point!@k@vS06/06/150600821. What matters is whether he has an alibi for the time of the crime!@k@vS06/06/150600822. ...Honestly!``
changeReason: New translation adds satoshi, satoshi-kun, altering character/location references.

TextENGNew: ``Oh... the police are coming to the house later, so I'll tell them I was with Satoshi at the festival.``
``...Sis?@k@vS06/06/150600824. A tacked-on lie won't help at all.@k@vS06/06/150600825. If you didn't lay any groundwork, it's too late for tricks now.``
``Just be honest with them.@k@vS06/06/150600827. Your acting is terrible, so trying too hard will only make them more suspicious.``
``...Yeah... sorry...``
Mion sounded thoroughly dejected.@kGive me a break -- I was the one who should have been depressed.
Sis usually managed well enough, but she lacked the quick thinking to handle sudden situations.
She was supposed to be the next head of the Sonozaki family. If this was the best she could do, the future looked bleak.
She was probably running out of things to say and getting ready to change the subject,@kand sure enough, right on cue, she deflected.
changeReason: Old 'Mion must have been embarrassed' misinterprets JPN 返事にも窮してきて ('running out of things to say'); new translation correctly conveys she was struggling to respond.

TextENGNew: ``Shion, you haven't been able to work much lately, have you...?``
``How astute of you.@k@vS06/06/150600829. Thanks to you.``
``Oh... right, sorry about that...@k@vS06/03/150300184. ...I'll stay out of sight until tonight, so maybe you could go to your shift...?``
Between all the preparatory meetings and arrangements for the Watanagashi, Sis had been constantly visible in public lately.
changeReason: New translation adds watanagashi, altering character/location references.

TextENGNew: When Mion was out and about, I couldn't pretend to be her.
Which meant I couldn't go to work.
For someone who had to earn her own living expenses, that was a serious blow.
Truthfully, I didn't feel like working.
...But my finances weren't robust enough to let me lounge around savoring my ennui.
As I was locking my apartment door, the sound must have alerted him, because the door of the neighboring unit opened and Kasai peeked out.
``Shion-san, are you heading out...?``

``Something like that.@k@vS06/06/150600831. Sis is going to stay hidden for me, so I'm finally going back to work.@k@vS06/06/150600832. Kasai, if you're free, would you mind driving me to my uncle's shop?``
changeReason: New translation adds kasai, altering character/location references.

TextENGNew: ``...Shion-san, wouldn't it be wiser to hold off on work for a little while?@k@vS06/15/151500148. The police seem to be watching the Sonozaki family's territory because of the incident.@k@vS06/15/151500149. It wouldn't be prudent to go walking around as 'Mion' right now.``
changeReason: New translation adds mion, sonozaki, altering character/location references.

TextENGNew: ``I appreciate the concern, but if I don't work, I can't eat.``
Saying something like that risked Kasai offering to cover my meals himself.
But not accepting money from Kasai was a rule I'd set when I started this life -- a firm vow.
I might not follow anyone else's rules, but precisely because of that, I clung stubbornly to the ones I made for myself.
Kasai knew that once I'd made up my mind, nothing would change it.
After a few perfunctory attempts to get me to reconsider, he easily relented and went to get the car ready.
Whenever we drove to my uncle's shop, we invariably got caught at this particular traffic light.
Even knowing it was inevitable, being stopped by it yet again was still irritating.
Pedestrians shuffled across the crosswalk in front of us.
Among them, I was startled to spot someone who looked exactly like Satoshi-kun.
...It was unmistakably him.
Although I'd spoken with him on the phone just recently, I hadn't seen him in person for ages.
``Kasai.@k@vS06/06/150600835. Sorry, but could you tell my uncle I won't be coming in today after all?@k@vS06/06/150600836. I'm getting out here.``
Kasai called after me in bewilderment, but I was already out of the car.
The traffic light turned green.
Kasai seemed to be shouting something in my direction, but the car behind him was leaning on the horn, and he had no choice but to drive on.
Satoshi-kun was on his bicycle, but he was going so slowly it was barely faster than walking, so I caught up easily and tapped him on the back.
``Helloooo.@k@vS06/06/150600838. Running into you in Okinomiya -- what a coincidence, Satoshi-kun.``
If I spoke to him with the biggest smile I could muster,@kmaybe he'd smile back just as brightly.
...With that hope, I put on my very best smile.
His face still bore heavy signs of exhaustion.
But within that expression, I could sense his willingness to return even a fraction of my smile.@k...My heart instantly felt lighter, as if a weight had been lifted.
I couldn't see my own face without a mirror, but I could feel the color rushing back to my cheeks.
changeReason: Major rewrite with altered negation, potentially changing meaning.

TextENGNew: If even I could tell, then to Satoshi-kun standing beside me, I must have looked deliriously happy.
``I don't know what happened, but... you seem to be in a good mood, Mion.``
``Of course I am.@k@vS06/06/150600840. You look like you're doing better, so my mood just followed yours.``
I said it with a hint of playful teasing, narrowing my eyes and smiling, and Satoshi-kun returned an expression that was just slightly bashful.
...That kind of reaction brought back memories of our happiest days together.
Which only made my mood soar even higher.
``What about you, Satoshi-kun -- did something happen?@k@vS06/06/150600842. Something good?``
``...Nothing good, really.@k@vS06/07/151200283. ...My aunt passed away, and... dealing with the police and the neighbors is a lot.``
For a moment, I thought I'd put my foot in it again and would crush his spirits.
But that fear turned out to be completely unfounded.
There was still a shadow on the face that answered me, but beneath it, a gentle smile was unmistakably present.
It was as if... he'd just completed some monumental task. Exhaustion and accomplishment, intertwined.
That was the feeling I sensed.
And so... I became certain.
He was the one who killed the wicked aunt who had tormented Satoko.
I had no evidence whatsoever, but I knew it intuitively.
So I skipped over all of that.
I already understood his motive for killing the aunt, how cornered he'd been to reach that point.
The circumstances surrounding him, the environment... and Satoshi-kun's feelings.@k...I understood all of it.
So I skipped over all of that.
Wanting simply to congratulate him on being freed from that curse-like burden, I smiled as if nothing were different and asked:
``Where are you headed, Satoshi-kun?@k@vS06/06/150600844. Oh, work?``
``Got it in one.@k@vS06/07/151200285. ...I have a quick stop to make first, though.``
``Ahahahaha...@k@vS06/07/151200287. I need to check the display case every day to make sure it's still there... otherwise I can't relax.``
Those words triggered a chain of memories.
The reason Satoshi-kun had started working part-time in the first place.
changeReason: New translation adds satoshi, satoshi-kun; removes satoko, altering character/location references.

TextENGNew: It was because Satoko wanted a huge stuffed animal from the toy store just up the road.
changeReason: New translation adds satoko, altering character/location references.

TextENGNew: And the thing apparently wasn't cheap.
Satoko's birthday was supposedly right after the Watanagashi Festival.
...I didn't know the exact date.
Wait -- hadn't he said he'd already quit his job?
Maybe he was going to thank the people he'd worked for, or clean out a locker he'd been using. Something like that.
Either way, Satoshi-kun had saved up his target amount and would soon be completely free of his part-time life.
changeReason: New translation adds satoshi, satoshi-kun, altering character/location references.

TextENGNew: 'I need to make sure it's still there' -- that suggested he hadn't received his final pay yet.@kGiven how things looked, he might be getting it right around Satoko's birthday.
When the toy store came into view, Satoshi-kun broke into a jog and pressed his face against the display window.
He confirmed it hadn't been sold, and visibly relaxed.
``Good, good.@k@vS06/07/151200289. ...All right.@k@vS06/07/151200290. It's still there.``
``You've earned enough money to buy it, right?@k@vS06/06/150600847. Why not just put down a reservation?``
The concept of reserving it apparently hadn't even occurred to him.
...Once again, I was struck by how desperately Satoshi-kun needed someone looking after him.
I leaned over his shoulder and peered into the display case.
By my standards, it wasn't all that cute, but it was undeniably enormous -- an oversized stuffed animal crammed uncomfortably into the case.
It looked less like merchandise and more like part of the store's decor.
``There's no price tag. Is this really for sale?@k@vS06/06/150600849. How much does it even cost?``
``...Mm, yeah.@k@vS06/07/151200293. I already confirmed they're selling it. Definitely.``
``And the price?@k@vS06/06/150600851. Will your budget cover it?``
``It'll be fine. I already checked, really...``
Apparently deciding he was being made fun of, Satoshi-kun pouted, puffing out his cheeks.
``If the budget's fine, then there's no problem.@k@vS06/06/150600853. Let's go reserve it right now.@k@vS06/06/150600854. That way you can stop worrying.``
We entered the slightly dusty shop and looked around for a clerk.
``...Is anyone here...?``
``Ahahahaha, perfect.@k@vS06/06/150600856. Satoshi-kun, you might get the stuffed animal without even waiting for payday.``
``W-We can't, Mion. Stealing is wrong...``
``...I'm joking.@k@vS06/06/150600856_2. I was hoping you'd just laugh that off.@k@vS06/06/150600856_3. ...Wait, do you actually think I'm the kind of girl who'd casually shoplift?``
``Oh... sorry. Ahahahaha...``
Satoshi-kun laughed sheepishly, as though trying to smooth things over.
I returned his attempt with an exaggerated pout.
This kind of back-and-forth... felt achingly nostalgic.
It felt like so many unpleasant things, so many dreary things, so many things I wanted to forget had accumulated before today.
...But all of that had vanished as cleanly as words written in sand, wiped away by a single wave.
Behind the register, I spotted a rocking chair where an elderly person appeared to be napping.
We'd made quite a ruckus, but the fact that they still hadn't woken up suggested very deep sleep.
``Excuse me...@k@vS06/06/150600861. You work here, don't you?``
I tried poking their shoulder, but there was no sign of waking.
Just then, a woman in an apron came in announcing 'I'm back!'

``Grandpa! I asked you to mind the shop and you fell asleep!@k@vS06/00/omoA15002. We have customers and everything!@k@vS06/00/omoA15003. I'm so sorry about this!@k@vS06/00/omoA15004. One moment, please!``
The woman shook the old man awake with somewhat rough handling and told him to go back to the living quarters.
Then, with an apologetic smile, she moved behind the register.
``Sorry you had to see that.@k@vS06/00/omoA15006. How can I help you?``
``Oh, I don't want to buy it yet -- I'd like to reserve something.@k@vS06/07/151200300. The biggest stuffed animal in the display window.``
``Oh, that one?@k@vS06/00/omoA15008. Are you sure?@k@vS06/00/omoA15010. It's quite expensive. Can you really afford it?``
``Oh... yes.@k@vS06/07/151200302. I should just barely have enough.``
``He's getting his paycheck very soon, so he'd like to reserve it until then...``
The shopkeeper looked mildly inconvenienced, but she jotted down Satoshi-kun's name and accepted the reservation.
Satoshi-kun received a note serving as his reservation slip.
``See?@k@vS06/06/150600863. Now you don't have to worry about someone else buying it.``
``Yeah.@k@vS06/07/151200305. ...My paycheck's coming any day now, too... I feel so relieved.``
At this rate, Satoshi-kun would have kept coming to check the display case every single day.
Agonizing over whether it was still there today, whether it would be there tomorrow. Pointless worry upon pointless worry.
Freed from that mental burden, Satoshi-kun wore a genuinely relieved smile, savoring the simple joy of having the reservation slip in hand.
``I should've done this sooner.``
``...Most people reserve things the moment they decide they want them.@k@vS06/06/150600865. The fact that you didn't think of that makes you a rare specimen, Satoshi-kun.``
changeReason: New translation adds satoshi, satoshi-kun, altering character/location references.

TextENGNew: ``Mm... mmph.``
I was remembering just how fun it was to tease him.
...All those dark, painful, lonely days until yesterday felt like they'd been a lie.
Satoshi-kun, too, seemed intent on making everything up to yesterday into a lie.
``Anyway... thanks.``
Satoshi-kun said it as he reached his hand toward my head.
...The moment I realized he was going to pat my head, I could feel my face flushing on its own.
His hand hadn't even touched me yet.@kBut the top of my head was already flooding with that ticklish, warm feeling, remembering all those days when he'd casually patted it.
...The fact that a gruesome murder had occurred just yesterday was already of no interest whatsoever to the person I was right now.
If the root cause of unhappiness for Satoshi-kun and Satoko was dead, then that was fine.
changeReason: New translation adds satoko, altering character/location references.

TextENGNew: If happy days were being restored from today onward -- for Satoshi-kun, and for me -- then that was more than enough.
changeReason: New translation adds satoshi, satoshi-kun, altering character/location references.

TextENGNew: ...But no matter how long I waited, Satoshi-kun's hand never patted my head.
I gently opened my tightly shut eyes.
And... discovered that the situation had completely transformed.
Four men in suits.
It was as though they'd been lying in wait for us to exit the toy store.
``Hello, hello.@k@vS06/11/150700107. Nfu fu fu fu fu...``
The oldest of the men stepped forward with a thoroughly unpleasant grin.
I'd never met him before, but from the atmosphere, he seemed to know both Mion and Satoshi-kun.
He looked like a police detective.
I rapidly searched through the information Sis had recently given me.@k...A match came up instantly.
``Good afternoon.@k@vS06/11/150700109. Houjou-san, Sonozaki-san.@k@vS06/11/150700110. Sorry to interrupt such a pleasant scene.``
Ooishi Kuraudo.
...A detective from the Okinomiya precinct, investigating the series of incidents.
changeReason: New translation adds okinomiya, altering character/location references.

TextENGNew: Apparently he suspected the Sonozaki family of being behind the chain of mysterious deaths.@kHe was the family's most prominent adversary.
He had deep ties to the prefectural police's Investigation Division 4, and was particularly well-versed in the local underworld.
changeReason: New translation changes numerical values (old: set(), new: {'4'}).

TextENGNew: ...Even without the mysterious deaths, his interference with the Sonozaki family's less savory business dealings made him plenty of an enemy.
And he was certainly no friend to Mion, the family's next head.
I began simulating how Sis would respond.

``...Well, well, if it isn't Ooishi-san.@k@vS06/06/150600867. Hard at work, I see.@k@vS06/06/150600868. Four grown men gathered at a toy store -- shopping for someone?@k@vS06/06/150600869. Ahahahaha!``
Needless to say, their business wasn't with the toy store. It was with us.
Not with me, in all likelihood.@k...With Satoshi-kun.
I glanced at Satoshi-kun... He wasn't showing an extreme reaction, but he was visibly unsettled.
The other detectives had subtly closed in around us, as though anticipating an escape attempt.
``Houjou-san.@k@vS06/11/150700112. The thing is, I have a few questions I'd like to ask you.``
``What's this about?@k@vS06/06/150600872. Is this one of those 'voluntary accompaniments'?@k@vS06/06/150600873. Satoshi-kun, you don't have to go along with this. Let's go!``
I gave Satoshi-kun a firm nudge on the shoulder.@kBut he just stood there, face drained of color, his mind clearly elsewhere.
Elsewhere?
No -- it looked more like resignation. As if he'd accepted his fate.
...At that moment,@kI cursed Sis's incompetence.
You don't even need to see the crime scene to know who killed the aunt -- it's obvious it was Satoshi-kun!
changeReason: New translation adds satoshi, satoshi-kun, altering character/location references.

TextENGNew: How could she have failed to create an alibi for him?!
That fool Mion had probably already told the police everything about that night exactly as it happened.
Which meant she'd honestly told them she had no idea where Satoshi-kun was!
If Sis hadn't taken any action, no villager would go out of their way to cover for him.
Put it all together, and it would become glaringly obvious that Satoshi-kun was the only person without an alibi.
The police had been methodically closing the net, and now they were going to press him with this 'voluntary accompaniment' and wrench out a confession.
...I looked at Satoshi-kun's face one more time.
There wasn't a shred of fighting spirit left in it.
Satoshi-kun probably didn't even know he had the right to refuse a voluntary accompaniment.
He'd meekly follow them to the interrogation room, and the moment this wily old fox Ooishi applied the slightest bit of pressure, he'd cave and confess everything.
...Satoshi-kun simply couldn't manage without someone looking out for him.
But nobody had.@k...Then I had to be the one to protect him.
But how?!
Reverse everything Mion had already told them, claiming it was all a misunderstanding?@kThat would be utterly futile at this point.
If I'd had just a bit more time to think, my cunning brain would surely have devised a clever way out.
But there was no time for such luxury.
The detectives were inching closer, building unmistakable pressure.
What I perceived merely as physical proximity, Satoshi-kun must have been feeling as crushing psychological force.
While I sat here paralyzed, trying to think of a miracle play, Satoshi-kun might crack under the tension and surrender.
Before thinking -- something, anything, anything!
I can't stay silent. I can't.
Before this silence crushes Satoshi-kun.
I have to do something to block this checkmate...!!
Ooishi's massive frame bore down on Satoshi-kun.
changeReason: New translation adds satoshi, satoshi-kun, altering character/location references.

TextENGNew: Overwhelmed, Satoshi-kun stumbled a step backward.
``It won't take much of your time.@k@vS06/11/150700114. Come now, there's nothing to be afraid of.``
``...Nfu fu fu fu fu!``
'It won't take much time' -- he says!@kHe just made it crystal clear!@kHe just said that once he gets his hands on Satoshi-kun, he'll never let him go!!
Satoshi-kun, you must not give in! If you break here, everything you've endured up to now will have been for nothing!!
Satoshi-kun's groan was a sign that his thread of composure was about to snap, and Ooishi had surely read that even more keenly than I had...
Ooishi grinned and extended his arm toward Satoshi-kun's shoulder...
The instant I saw that, lightning shot through my spine.
That hand must not touch him.
The moment those blunt, merciless fingers closed around Satoshi-kun's shoulder, the sensation would break his will...!
changeReason: New translation adds satoshi, satoshi-kun; removes oishi, ooishi, altering character/location references.

TextENGNew: Satoshi-kun would nod weakly, and rather than merely complying with the accompaniment, he'd confess to everything on the spot...!!
changeReason: New translation adds satoshi, satoshi-kun, altering character/location references.

TextENGNew: ``You want to know whether Satoshi-kun has an alibi, right?``
Ooishi turned to me with a quizzical sound.@k...His hand, still reaching for Satoshi-kun, froze in mid-air.
changeReason: New translation adds satoshi, satoshi-kun, altering character/location references.

TextENGNew: ...I hadn't said it with any plan in mind.
All that mattered was stopping Ooishi's hand.
...It was nothing more than a stalling tactic.
Truly, completely, a bluff pulled from thin air.
``...That's right.@k@vS06/11/150700118. I want to verify Houjou Satoshi-san's alibi.``
changeReason: New translation adds satoshi, satoshi-san, altering character/location references.

TextENGNew: ``Nah ha ha... Maybe our investigation's just sloppy, but we simply cannot confirm Houjou-san's whereabouts during the time of the crime.``
changeReason: New translation adds houjou, houjou-san, altering character/location references.

TextENGNew: ``So, embarrassing as it is, we thought we'd ask the man himself directly.``
``You don't need to ask him.@k@vS06/06/150600876. I can vouch for his alibi.``
``Oh,@k@vS06/11/150700122. really?@k@vS06/11/150700123. That would be a great help.``
``You've already told us -- and many others have confirmed -- that you were at the shrine for the festival that night and stayed until the very end.``

``If you're now telling us that Houjou-san was also with you there, that would be the simplest resolution.@k@vS06/11/150700126. ...But the last time we asked you about the night in question, you never once mentioned Houjou-san.``
``Are you saying he was actually there all along and you're only remembering now?@k@vS06/11/150700128. That no one at the festival saw Houjou-san, but you alone did?``
``That's right.@k@vS06/06/150600878. He was with me. That should be sufficient.``
``...I know you were with your close friends the entire time. I observed that myself at the venue.``
``And you're still claiming you were also with him?@k@vS06/11/150700131. Nah ha ha ha...@k@vS06/11/150700132. Don't you think that's a bit of a stretch?@k@vS06/11/150700133. Sonozaki Mion-san?``
changeReason: New translation adds mion, mion-san, sonozaki, altering character/location references.

TextENGNew: Ooishi leaned in close with a spiteful smirk.
He wasn't toying with me for making desperate excuses... no, that wasn't it.
His naked irritation was that of a fisherman who'd been about to land a catch, only to have someone interfere at the last moment.
``Are you saying you were with him the whole time at the festival but completely forgot, and only now, conveniently, remembered?@k@vS06/11/150700133_2. Sonozaki Mion-san?``
A part of me thought 'this is bad' for a split second, but I couldn't stop myself anymore.
``Actually, I'm not Mion.@k@vS06/06/150600882. So I was never at the Watanagashi in the first place.``

``On the night of the Watanagashi, Satoshi-kun and I were talking the whole time at a family restaurant in Okinomiya.@k@vS06/06/150600884. The place is called Angel Mort. Feel free to check with the manager.``
changeReason: New translation adds watanagashi, altering character/location references.

TextENGNew: Ooishi still had his smirk plastered on, but there was no doubt he was confused.
``...What are you talking about?@k@vS06/11/150700136. If you're not Mion, then who are you?``
``Sonozaki... Shion.@k@vS06/06/150600886. Mion's twin sister.@k@vS06/06/150600887. I believe this is the first time we've met. How do you do?``
changeReason: New translation removes oishi, ooishi, ooishi-san, altering character/location references.

TextENGNew: Ooishi wore the face of a man trying to figure out what kind of desperate nonsense I was spouting.
But since I was delivering this outrageous claim with perfect composure, his bemused smile gradually hardened into displeasure.
changeReason: Old translation reduces an entire descriptive sentence to just 'He frowned,' omitting the JPN detail about delivering an outrageous claim with composure and the smile hardening to displeasure.

TextENGNew: ``This is the first I've heard of Sonozaki Mion having a twin sister.@k@vS06/11/150700138. ...Are you trying to make a fool of me, young lady?``
``Not at all.@k@vS06/06/150600889. Please feel free to check the family register, or whatever you like.@k@vS06/06/150600890. Or shall I call my sister and we can stand side by side for you?``
At that point, even the detectives couldn't hide their agitation.
``...Sonozaki... Shion-san?``
``That's right.@k@vS06/06/150600892. Shion. Is something the matter?``
``...Would you mind coming along as well?@k@vS06/11/150700141. If you're claiming to be Sonozaki Shion and that you were with Houjou-san, then I'd like to hear your account too.``
changeReason: New translation adds shion, sonozaki, altering character/location references.

TextENGNew: ``Gladly.@k@vS06/06/150600894. Please do go easy on us.``
The detectives huddled around their car, apparently having a heated discussion.
...The sudden appearance of a wild card named 'Shion' was clearly something they'd never anticipated.
changeReason: New translation adds shion, altering character/location references.

TextENGNew: But this should buy enough room to obscure Satoshi-kun's alibi somewhat.
I'd already given that idiot sister of mine an earful, so retroactively constructing an alibi where Satoshi-kun was with 'Shion' wasn't impossible.
changeReason: New translation adds satoshi, satoshi-kun, shion, altering character/location references.

TextENGNew: ``Satoshi-kun.@k@vS06/06/150600896. No matter what they ask, insist that you were chatting with me at Angel Mort the whole time.``

``I'll handle the rest.@k@vS06/06/150600898. If they press you on details, just say you don't remember clearly... Actually, for someone like you, Satoshi-kun, it might be safer to just stay quiet altogether.``
Satoshi-kun was standing there with his mouth hanging open.
...Even though I was telling him something crucial, it seemed to be going in one ear and out the other.
``Are you listening, Satoshi-kun?@k@vS06/06/150600900. If you don't hold it together here, you'll be playing right into their hands.``
``Oh... o-okay.``
Satoshi-kun... must have felt like the rug had been pulled out from under him.
Which was understandable.@kThe person he'd always believed was Mion had just revealed herself to be her twin sister.
The fact that I'd been impersonating Mion and deceiving Satoshi-kun all this time was an undeniable truth.
changeReason: New translation adds mion, satoshi, satoshi-kun, altering character/location references.

TextENGNew: ``...Are you angry...?``
``...Why would I be?``
``Because... I've been deceiving you.``
``...Aha ha ha ha ha ha.``
Satoshi-kun suddenly broke into a bright, open laugh.
Satoshi-kun wasn't the kind of person who could laugh to deflect an awkward situation.@kSo why he was laughing, I couldn't quite understand.
``Sometimes what you said in class didn't quite match up with what Mion said, and I always felt something was off.@k@vS06/07/151200316. ...Now it makes sense.``
changeReason: New translation adds mion, altering character/location references.

TextENGNew: ``...You're not upset?@k@vS06/06/150600904. About the deception...?``
Satoshi-kun tilted his head slightly.
...Being a bit slow on the uptake, he seemed genuinely unsure whether this was something he should be angry about.@k...Or perhaps he was choosing to act that way.
``So this isn't our first time meeting, right...?``
``Um... no... that's right...``
